metadata.title: MoStar Phantom POE — Formal vs Hidden Routes
metadata.description: 17 phantom corridors + 17 formal counterparts + nodes + crossing points
metadata.provenance: Corridor geographies from WHO AFRO, UNHCR, IOM field reports. Flow data from IOM DTM published reports.

properties.name: Mbandaka → Impfondo
properties.description: PHANTOM: Hidden/undocumented route. 133km gap with no formal monitoring.

properties.name: Gbadolite → Bangui
properties.description: PHANTOM: Hidden/undocumented route. 196km gap with no formal monitoring.

properties.name: Cazombo → Kazumba
properties.description: PHANTOM: Hidden/undocumented route. 120km gap with no formal monitoring.

properties.description: PHANTOM: Hidden/undocumented route. 80km gap with no formal monitoring.

properties.description: PHANTOM: Hidden/undocumented route. 210km gap with no formal monitoring.

properties.description: PHANTOM: Hidden/undocumented route. 196km gap with no formal monitoring.

properties.description: PHANTOM: Hidden/undocumented route. 147km gap with no formal monitoring.

properties.description: PHANTOM: Hidden/undocumented route. 108.5km gap with no formal monitoring.

properties.description: PHANTOM: Hidden/undocumented route. 2.8km gap with no formal monitoring.

properties.name: Baga → Diffa
properties.description: PHANTOM: Hidden/undocumented route. 102.89999999999999km gap with no formal monitoring.

properties.description: PHANTOM: Hidden/undocumented route. 36.4km gap with no formal monitoring.

properties.description: PHANTOM: Hidden/undocumented route. 7km gap with no formal monitoring.

properties.description: PHANTOM: Hidden/undocumented route. 5.6km gap with no formal monitoring.

properties.description: PHANTOM: Hidden/undocumented route. 368.9km gap with no formal monitoring.

properties.name: Kapoeta → Lodwar
properties.description: PHANTOM: Hidden/undocumented route. 171.5km gap with no formal monitoring.

properties.name: Damasak–Diffa Highway (N1)
properties.gap_note: Covers highway only. 147km of Lake Chad shoreline is completely unmonitored.
properties.description: FORMAL: Official Nigeria-Niger highway border crossing. Customs, immigration, military checkpoint. Controlled by Nigerian Army/MNJTF since 2015.

properties.name: Pulka–Mora Road (R1)
properties.gap_note: Kerawa post covers road only. Entire Mandara Mountain highland trail (52km) is unmonitored.
properties.description: FORMAL: Official Nigeria-Cameroon road crossing through Mandara Mountains. Intermittently staffed.

properties.name: Banki Official Gate
properties.gap_note: Gate monitors road traffic. Market shadow routes (10km) bypass through adjacent bush paths.
properties.description: FORMAL: Official Nigeria-Cameroon border gate at Banki. UNHCR presence. IDP camp adjacent.

properties.name: Mbandaka–Impfondo River Customs
properties.gap_note: Customs only checks large commercial vessels at ports. 40+ pirogue landing points along 190km have ZERO health screening. This is the mpox spread route.
properties.description: FORMAL: Official Congo River port customs at Mbandaka and Impfondo. Large commercial vessels checked, pirogues ignored.

properties.name: Zongo–Bangui Ferry
properties.gap_note: Zongo ferry is the only formal crossing for 280km of Ubangi River. Shadow ferries operate at dozens of unmonitored points.
properties.description: FORMAL: Official DRC-CAR crossing via Zongo ferry terminal. Formal customs/immigration on both sides.

properties.name: Dilolo Railway Border Post
properties.gap_note: Single border post for 800+km of DRC-Angola border. 186,776 Angola returnees through Kazumba (120km north) — ZERO formal monitoring.
properties.description: FORMAL: Official DRC-Angola crossing at Dilolo. Railway customs point. Only formal post for entire Kasai-Angola border.

properties.name: Kasumbalesa–Chembe Road
properties.gap_note: 400km from actual Kasai displacement zone. Zero relevance to Luiza-Dilolo movement. The formal system literally does not see this corridor.
properties.description: FORMAL: Nearest formal DRC-Angola post is Kasumbalesa/Chembe — 400km south of Luiza. Irrelevant to Kasai movement.

properties.name: Pagak Border Crossing
properties.gap_note: Pagak covers 50km of road. The 280km Gambela-Malakal corridor uses wetland trails, seasonal flood paths, and river crossings with zero monitoring.
properties.description: FORMAL: Official Ethiopia-South Sudan crossing. IOM FMP present. But 280km corridor extends far beyond this single point.

properties.name: Doolow–Mandera Triangle
properties.gap_note: Doolow covers the bridge crossing only. The 210km corridor to Baidoa traverses al-Shabaab territory with zero formal presence.
properties.description: FORMAL: Official Ethiopia-Somalia-Kenya tri-border crossing. IOM FMP active at Doolow.

properties.name: Metema–Gallabat Official Crossing
properties.gap_note: Formal post captures highway traffic. Humera-Kassala corridor (155km north) uses Setit River fords — zero monitoring.
properties.description: FORMAL: Main Ethiopia-Sudan formal border post. IOM FMP. 33,000/month. BUT only covers the highway — Setit River fords are unmonitored.

properties.name: Grande Barrière (Goma–Gisenyi)
properties.gap_note: Grande Barrière handles documented crossers. Informal paths through lakeside settlements bypass it entirely. During M23 crises, thousands cross outside the gate.
properties.description: FORMAL: Official DRC-Rwanda crossing. One of the busiest in Africa. But at 4km wide, hundreds cross daily through side paths.

properties.name: Am Dafok Border Post
properties.gap_note: Am Dafok covers a single point on 300km of open border. Armed groups, displaced civilians, and traders use dozens of desert tracks. Zero health infrastructure.
properties.description: FORMAL: Only formal CAR-Sudan crossing. Intermittently staffed. 300km of open desert/scrub between Birao and Nyala is completely uncontrolled.

properties.name: Nimule–Elegu Highway
properties.gap_note: Nimule-Elegu is heavily monitored. But the Yambio-Bangui corridor (527km west) goes through Garamba forest — zero formal presence. Different route entirely.
properties.description: FORMAL: Main South Sudan-Uganda highway crossing. IOM FMP. 8,000/month. But Yambio-Bangui (527km) goes through forest, not highway.

properties.name: Renk–Joda Highway Crossing
properties.gap_note: Formal post captures highway. The 175km corridor to Ed Damazin uses river crossings and bush paths — half the actual volume bypasses Joda entirely.
properties.description: FORMAL: Official South Sudan-Sudan crossing. IOM FMP. 25,000/month since Sudan conflict. One of the busiest in East Africa.

properties.name: Nadapal/Lokichogio Road
properties.gap_note: Nadapal covers one road. The 245km Kapoeta-Lodwar cattle corridor uses pastoral tracks invisible to any checkpoint system.
properties.description: FORMAL: Informal Kenya-South Sudan crossing at Nadapal. IOM intermittent presence. Pastoral routes extend far beyond.

properties.name: Metema–Gallabat Highway Post
properties.gap_note: Formal post exists but IOM data shows only 5% of actual crossing volume goes through the gate. Phantom captures the other 95%.
properties.description: FORMAL: This IS the formal crossing. Phantom corridor shows that even here, 5% formal coverage means 95% crosses outside the gate.

properties.name: Bossaso Port / Coast Guard
properties.gap_note: Coast guard patrols near port only. Smuggler boats depart from beaches along 200km of coast. IOM Mixed Migration Centre tracks arrivals in Yemen — 300+/day.
properties.description: FORMAL: Puntland coast guard patrols Bossaso coast. But smuggler boats depart from dozens of beach points along 200km of coast.
